prompt: Please repair this small C17 harbor-height chart module. `make test` currently fails under `-Wall -Wextra -Werror`: tide.c lacks declarations for `isspace`, `isdigit`, `strtol`, and `strtod`; its old-style `tide_interp` definition declares `minute` as `long` even though tide.h declares it as `int`; chart.c lacks declarations for `strlen`, `memset`, and `memcpy`; and `tide_slot_label` is defined in tide.c but is undeclared for both chart.c and the tests. The compiler consequently reports implicit declarations, an argument/prototype mismatch, and pointer-from-integer conversions. Fix the production sources and public module headers so `make test` compiles, links, and passes. Do not alter parsing, interpolation, state-tag, or row-layout behavior. Do not modify test_tide.c, mintest.h, or the Makefile.
notes: 5C4 item 1: implicit-declaration triage. Authoring-time toolchain: gcc 16.1.1, -std=c17 -Wall -Wextra -Werror (implicit-function-declaration and int-conversion are errors by default on this gcc). Planted, all independent, all visible in one make -k pass (no chain reveals; dump in prompt captured verbatim, macro-expansion repeats trimmed): (1) tide.c missing <ctype.h> (isspace, isdigit); (2) tide.c missing <stdlib.h> (strtol, strtod); (3) chart.c missing <string.h> (strlen/memset/memcpy, with builtin-declaration-mismatch noise); (4) tide_slot_label defined in tide.c but never declared in tide.h — implicit decl in chart.c AND test_tide.c, plus the catalog's int-returning-pointer truncation error at both use sites (the test-file errors force the fix into tide.h; a local declaration in chart.c alone leaves the frozen test TU broken, and the prompt says the tests are frozen); (5) K&R-style definition of tide_interp whose 'long minute' param mismatches the prototype's int ('argument doesn't match prototype' — probed: a promotion-compatible K&R def compiles silently on this gcc, so the planted def must genuinely drift in type). Reference fix: two includes added, one include added, one declaration added to tide.h, tide_interp rewritten as a prototype-style definition with int minute. Behavior teeth: parse edge cases (hour/minute range, two-digit minutes, separators, missing height), interp clamping incl. span<=0, quarter-band label boundaries inclusive at both edges, byte-exact chart rows (pad/cut to 10 cols, %5.2f).
